channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Relatives say a whole family was killed in Israel's deadliest West Bank strike since Oct. 7
item.description: TULKAREM, West Bank (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a West Bank cafe that the military said targeted Palestinian militants also killed a family of four, including two young children, relatives told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: NASSER NASSER and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden: 'I don't know' if Netanyahu trying to influence election
item.description: President Biden on Friday said he wasn't aware of whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was trying to influence the U.S. election, while noting no administration has been a stronger ally to Israel than his own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: US launches airstrikes by fighter jets and ships on Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military struck more than a dozen Houthi targets in Yemen on Friday, going after weapons systems, bases and other equipment belonging to the Iranian-backed rebels, U.S. officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Argentine president delivered modified 'West Wing' monologue at UN General Assembly
item.description: Argentine President Javier Milei delivered a United Nations General Assembly address last month with remarkable similarities to a monologue recited by fictional U.S. President Jed Bartlet from “The West Wing." Toward the end of Milei’s Sept.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor and mentor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: TERESA DE MIGUEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US arranges flights to bring Americans out of Lebanon as others seek escape
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S.-arranged flights have brought about 350 Americans and their immediate relatives out of Lebanon this week during escalated fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, while thousands of others still there face airstrikes and diminishing commercial flights.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MATTHEW LEE and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Clashes in Kenya as people discuss the deputy president's impeachment motion
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Supporters and opponents of Kenya's deputy president clashed Friday at public forums over an impeachment motion against him, which was introduced in parliament by the ruling alliance this week.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's Ayatollah Khamenei defends missile barrage against Israel in rare sermon
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei said during a rare public speech Friday that his country's missile attack on Israel this week was "legal and legitimate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats suspect Netanyahu attempting to tilt Trump-Harris race
item.description: Democrats increasingly suspect Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is trying to interfere in U.S. domestic politics by ignoring President Biden's calls to negotiate a peace deal in Gaza and by confronting Hezbollah and Iran weeks before the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: The Nobel Prizes will be announced against a backdrop of wars, famine and artificial intelligence
item.description: STAVANGER, Norway (AP) — Wars, a refugee crisis, famine and artificial intelligence could all be recognized when Nobel Prize announcements begin next week under a shroud of violence. The prize week coincides with the Oct.
item.dc:creator: MARK LEWIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japan's new leader expresses regret for governing party slush fund scandal, vows stronger defense
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — New Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba opened his first policy speech in office Friday by expressing deep regrets over the governing party's slush funds scandal that dogged his predecessor.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tunisia votes Sunday in its third presidential election since the Arab Spring
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — With his major opponents imprisoned or left off the ballot, Tunisian President Kais Saied faces few obstacles to winning reelection on Sunday, five years after riding anti-establishment backlash to a first term. The North African country's Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump slams Biden for saying Israel shouldn't target Iranian nuclear sites: 'The craziest thing I've ever heard'
item.description: Former President Trump slammed President Biden for saying that Israel should not target Iranian nuclear sites, arguing the commander-in-chief's remark was "not the right answer." “I mean, that's the craziest thing I’ve ever heard. That's the biggest risk we have.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Peru celebrates 2 decades of a fast-growing breed of guinea pigs eaten as a delicacy
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru on Thursday celebrated two decades since the creation of a genetically modified breed of guinea pig, a rodent whose meat has formed a part of the diet of people in the Andean nation for thousands of years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: G20 environment ministers back funding for forest conservation
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Environment ministers of the Group of 20 nations agreed Thursday to support the creation of funding sources for ecosystem services, acknowledging Brazil's proposal to establish a trust fund for forest conservation.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know as Israel expands its operations in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli airstrikes hammered targets across southern Lebanon and Beirut's suburbs while its military urged residents to flee more than two dozen Lebanese towns and villages, further signs that Israel is ramping up its operations in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum described Thursday as “deplorable.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 78 people died when a boat with 278 on board capsized in eastern Congo, official says
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — At least 78 people died when an overcrowded boat capsized on Lake Kivu in eastern Congo on Thursday, a local governor said. A frantic search and rescue operation was underway hours later as many remained unaccounted for from the vessel, believed to have had 278 people on board.
item.dc:creator: RUTH ALONGA, JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian stronghold Vuhledar falls to Russian offensive after two years of bombardment
item.description: Vuhledar fell to Russian troops this week after Ukrainian forces were forced to withdraw to preserve lives and military equipment after more than two years of bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens of migrants still missing off Djibouti's coast after smugglers forced them out of boats
item.description: MOGADISHU, Somalia (AP) — Rescuers searched Thursday for dozens of migrants from Africa still missing after smugglers apparently forced them out of two boats in the Red Sea off the coast of Djibouti earlier this week, the Djiboutian coast guard said.
item.dc:creator: OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Harpole Treasure includes one of the most valuable pieces of ancient jewelry found in Britain
item.description: The Harpole Treasure was discovered in 2022, and refers to a collection of valuable relics unearthed from a burial ground in England. Among the finds was a unique piece of jewelry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yazidi woman held hostage for 10 years in Gaza rescued in Israel, US operation
item.description: A Yazidi woman held captive by a Hamas terrorist after she was abducted by ISIS a decade ago has been returned to her family after she escaped through coordinated efforts by Israeli and U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New NATO chief Mark Rutte visits Ukraine in his first trip since taking office
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — New NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte visited Ukraine on Thursday in his first official trip since taking office and pledging the alliance's continued support for Kyiv in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shooting near luxury Mexico resort leaves 1 dead, suspects flee on jet skis
item.description: A Mexican national was shot and killed Wednesday near a ritzy resort in Cancun before two suspects fled on jet skis, according to a local report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgian parliament speaker signs anti-LGBTQ law after president refuses to sign it
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — The speaker of the Georgian parliament signed into a law Thursday a bill that severely curtails LGBTQ+ rights in the country and mirrors legislation adopted in neighboring Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WikiLeaks’ Julian Assange says he pleaded ‘guilty to journalism’ in order to be freed
item.description: In Julian Assange's first remarks since his release from prison in June, the WikiLeaks founder said he was freed after 5 years of incarceration because he 'pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: US destroyer fires ballistic missile interceptors to defend Israel against Iranian barrage
item.description: The USS Cole and USS Bulkeley fired about a dozen ballistic missile interceptors to defend Israel from a barrage of nearly 200 Iranian missiles on Tuesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 Israeli soldiers killed in Lebanon as Netanyahu says IDF engaged in 'tough war' with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's military announced Wednesday that eight soldiers have died during "combat in southern Lebanon" as troops are wiping out Hezbollah infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ancient painting revealed in Egypt beneath layers of bird poop
item.description: During restoration of the Temple of Edfu, artwork was discovered beneath layers of bird poop, dust and soot covering the temple walls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico's army seizes local police weapons in cartel heartland amid gunfights, violence
item.description: The Mexican army has seized the firearms of local police in the Sinaloa city of Culiacan as cartel violence has escalated, dozens have been killed in the streets and distrust in the police grows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli mom killed in Jaffa terror attack while shielding 9-month-old son, Israel says
item.description: Inbar Segev-Vigder, a 33-year-old mother, died in the Jaffa terrorist attack while shielding her 9-month-old son from gunfire, Israel says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A likely Russian artillery strike kills at least 6 at a Ukrainian market
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An apparent Russian artillery strike hit a market in the southern Ukraine city of Kherson on Tuesday, killing at least six people and wounding three others, authorities said, on a morning when Ukrainians across the country were observing a minute’s silence for their military and
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The release of the video comes after a series of Russian incursions into the Alaska Air Defense Identification Zone.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli raids against Hezbollah signal a wider ground incursion into Lebanon could come soon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military launched small ground raids against Hezbollah and sealed off communities along its northern border on Monday as Israeli artillery pounded southern Lebanon and signals grew that more forces could soon be sent across the border to fight the Iran-backed militants.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, SAMY MAGDY, ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation
